title: Endpoint ready-valid service probe for Llama7B frontier
hypothesis: The selected endpoint full on-chip service frontier can be mapped to a concrete ready/valid endpoint RTL configuration with finite endpoint and bank queues, producer/consumer backpressure, and locality-first bank drain.

direct_comparison.primary_question: Does the current least-abstract Llama7B endpoint service point have a concrete ready/valid endpoint implementation for the selected packet and queue sizing?
include: Derive DATA_W from packet_payload_bytes and queue depths from endpoint_queue_depth_bytes and bank_queue_depth_bytes.
Run onchip_service_endpoint.sv with finite endpoint and bank queues.
Report accepted/emitted beats, producer stalls, consumer stalls, occupancy maxima, and final completion cycle.
exclude: Do not change HBM/DRAM bandwidth, frequency, or service policy.
Do not run a new Llama7B architecture search.
Do not claim full router or SRAM macro RTL equivalence from this endpoint-only probe.
Do not reintroduce abstract NoC bandwidth sweeps.
follow_on_broad_sweep: If the endpoint probe passes, use the endpoint service point as the current least-abstract on-chip frontier.
The next abstraction reductions are concrete router/SRAM composition and then HBM/DRAM service modeling.
If the probe fails, revise the endpoint queue policy before further scheduler search.

expected_benefit: Tie the current best endpoint service policy to concrete RTL ready/valid behavior.
Validate that finite endpoint and bank queues create measurable backpressure instead of remaining purely analytic.
Keep HBM/DRAM out of scope while reducing the on-chip endpoint abstraction.
risks: The probe covers endpoint buffering and bank queue behavior, not complete NoC router routing.
It does not include SRAM macro timing or physical closure for the derived 1024-bit endpoint datapath.
The traffic pattern is a focused stress probe rather than a full Llama7B trace replay.

expected_result.reason: The result should pass the endpoint ready/valid probe and report concrete derived RTL parameters.
